steps.title: Identify the key contrast
steps.content: The passage centers on one decisive contrast: critics warned that writing in Gikuyu would limit Ngugi's reach, yet he went on to achieve widespread acclaim with works translated into more than thirty languages.

steps.title: Match the contrast to the choices
steps.content:
Choice B (‘Although Ngugi's decision to write in Gikuyu was met with some skepticism, it didn't prevent him from achieving literary success.’) uses an “Although…didn't prevent” structure that directly mirrors this contrast, showing skepticism didn't hinder his success.

The other three either isolate facts without the skepticism backdrop, wrongly claim Gikuyu is widely understood, or focus on just the decision without the outcome.

This confirms B is correct.
